raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1460 protects access to the federal 340B discount drug program for clinics and health centers.",
    "Pharmaceutical manufacturers started restricting access to the 340B program during the pandemic.",
    "AB 1460 prohibits manufacturers from using discriminatory practices that limit access to the 340B program.",
    "AB 1460 helps community health centers and clinics address health equity and access to care issues in California.",
    "Contract pharmacy restrictions limited access to the 340B program in mid-2020.",
    "Discriminatory practices make it harder for covered entities to leverage contract pharmacies for discounts on medications for patients.",
    "The 340B program was founded in 1992 to help providers that serve vulnerable and uninsured patients secure affordable prescription drugs.",
    "Covered entities are allowed to dispense discounted medications to uninsured and insured patients under the 340B program.",
    "Discount medications can be provided at cost to uninsured patients, making lifesaving medications more affordable.",
    "Covered entities retain the difference between the reduced price paid for drugs and the full amount reimbursed by patient insurance, which funds important services."
  ]
}
```
